---
title: "Thinking like a computer scientist"
author: "Gary Napier gary.napier@ccn.ac.uk"
format:
  revealjs:
    smaller: true
    logo: img/ccn_logo.png
    css: ../quarto/slides_styles.css
    footer: "Access to Computing - Unix/Linux and command line"
    embed-resources: true
---


# Problem solving

# Levels of problem solving

[](screenshots/problem_solving_dialogue.png)
[](screenshots/problem_solving_flowchart.png)
[](screenshots/problem_solving_formal.png)
[](screenshots/problem_solving_hierarchy.png)
[](screenshots/problem_solving_mess.png)
[](screenshots/problem_solving_problem.png)
[](screenshots/problem_solving_problems_to_puzzles.png)
[](screenshots/problem_solving_puzzle.png)
[](screenshots/problem_solving_topology.png)

# Tenacity

# Google searching

# Creativity 

# Collaboration   

# AI

# Resources

[How to think like a computer scientist](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sVUda0o98GQ) short video

[How to think like a Computer Scientist](https://runestone.academy/ns/books/published/thinkcspy/index.html) - interactive book with Python code

The Strategy Unit, Steve Wyatt - [An introduction to problem structuring](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-X09nvNQkYg) (~50 mins)

[Elements and Principles of Data Analysis](https://www.researchgate.net/publication/331888135_Elements_and_Principles_of_Data_Analysis)
